title: NOC Live Widget + KD Trust Favorites Backend Persistence

requirements.text: Add a stable, upgrade-safe per-user favorites data structure to the Motoko backend actor. Use a stable HashMap keyed by user Principal to store each user's list of favorite AI tool names as Text arrays. Ensure state survives canister upgrades via stable variables.

quotes: KD Trust Favorites Panel with backend persistence

acceptanceCriteria: A stable variable (e.g., stable var favoritesStore) of type HashMap<Principal, [Text]> exists in the actor
State is preserved across canister upgrades
The data structure is keyed by caller Principal

requirements.text: Implement a `getFavorites` query method in the Motoko actor that identifies the caller via `msg.caller` and returns that caller's stored list of favorite tool names as `[Text]`. Return an empty array if no favorites are stored for the caller.
acceptanceCriteria: Method signature: public query func getFavorites() : async [Text]
Returns stored favorites for the calling principal
Returns [] for principals with no stored favorites

requirements.text: Implement a `setFavorites` update method in the Motoko actor that accepts a `[Text]` argument (the full list of favorite tool names), identifies the caller via `msg.caller`, and overwrites the caller's stored favorites with the provided list.
acceptanceCriteria: Method signature: public func setFavorites(favorites: [Text]) : async ()
Overwrites the caller's existing favorites with the new list
An empty array call clears the caller's favorites

requirements.text: Update the frontend `useKDTrustFavorites` hook (frontend/src/hooks/useKDTrustFavorites.ts) to call the backend `getFavorites` and `setFavorites` actor methods for authenticated users. On login, merge any locally stored favorites from localStorage with backend favorites, persist the merged list to the backend, and clear localStorage. Unauthenticated users continue to use localStorage only.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users' favorites are loaded from the backend on mount
Toggling a favorite calls setFavorites on the backend for authenticated users
On login, localStorage favorites are merged with backend favorites and localStorage is cleared
Unauthenticated users read/write favorites to localStorage only

requirements.text: Add a live NOC mini-widget to the KD Trust page (frontend/src/pages/KDTrustPage.tsx) that displays real-time system status for Omni-Mesh, AI Self-Healing, Black Hole Mode, and Predictive Failover. Since WebSockets are not supported on the platform, use a polling interval of 5 seconds via React Query or setInterval to fetch NOC status from the existing backend actor. Each status badge must glow and pulse using CSS keyframe animations, with color-coded values: ACTIVE=green, IDLE=gray, INACTIVE=red, READY=yellow, NOT READY=orange. Apply smooth CSS transitions on status changes.

quotes: Live NOC Mini-Widget with glowing pulsing indicators
Glowing, pulsing badges for each system status

acceptanceCriteria: A NOC mini-widget is visible on the KD Trust page
Omni-Mesh, AI Self-Healing, Black Hole Mode, and Predictive Failover statuses are displayed
Each status badge glows and pulses via CSS keyframe animation
Status colors match: ACTIVE=green, IDLE=gray, INACTIVE=red, READY=yellow, NOT READY=orange
Status values update every 5 seconds via polling (no WebSocket)
CSS transitions animate badge changes smoothly

constraints: WebSockets are not supported on this platform — use polling (setInterval or React Query refetchInterval) for live NOC status updates instead
Do not edit any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo)
getFavorites and setFavorites must use msg.caller (Principal) as the key, not a userId Text parameter
nonGoals: Building a full KD control room dashboard grid with multiple NOC widgets and metrics panels
Adding new AI tools or changing the existing aiTools data source
Changing the overall visual theme of the KD Trust page beyond the NOC widget
